hero.headline: NMIMS Online MBA in Business Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved specialisation. 16 spec subjects across Sem 3-4. NIRF #52, Mumbai heritage brand.

sections.tldr: NMIMS Online MBA in Business Management covers corporate finance, consumer behaviour, organisational design, supply chain, cybersecurity, digital product design, business process transformation, AI concepts, and agile methodology across 16 Sem 3-4 subjects. NIRF #52 at Rs 1,96,000. Best for general management professionals and technology-adjacent business roles targeting cross-functional leadership at modern organisations.

about.body:
Business Management at NMIMS is the most forward-looking general management specialisation among NMIMS Online MBA options. While traditional general management programmes cover strategy, HR, marketing, and finance in a cross-functional framework, the NMIMS Business Management spec adds a significant technology management layer: cybersecurity and governance, digital product design, business process transformation, AI concepts, and agile methodology. This positioning reflects the reality that modern business management roles increasingly require technology fluency alongside classical management skills.

NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) has built this spec for senior professionals and mid-career managers at technology-adjacent companies, digital transformation roles in traditional industries, and business leaders in the intersection of management and technology. It is the right choice for professionals at companies where business strategy and technology strategy are increasingly the same conversation.

Full programme overview at /universities/nmims-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Corporate Finance
sem3Subjects.description: Build financial decision-making skills for business managers: budget management, capital allocation, ROI evaluation, and cost-benefit analysis.

sem3Subjects.name: Research Methodology
sem3Subjects.description: Apply quantitative and qualitative research methods to business management problems with structured hypothesis testing and analysis frameworks.

sem3Subjects.name: Project Pre-work
sem3Subjects.description: Define dissertation scope, literature review structure, and research questions for the Sem 4 business management research project.

sem3Subjects.name: Information Systems for Management
sem3Subjects.description: Evaluate enterprise information systems: ERP, CRM, and decision support systems for operational and strategic management applications.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Behaviour
sem3Subjects.description: Analyse psychological and social drivers of purchase decisions, buyer journey mapping, and consumer insights for business strategy.

sem3Subjects.name: Organisational Theory, Structure and Design
sem3Subjects.description: Design organisational structures for different growth stages, manage reporting relationships, and build adaptive organisations.

sem3Subjects.name: Supply Chain Management
sem3Subjects.description: Optimise supply chain operations, manage vendor relationships, and build resilient sourcing and distribution networks for business continuity.

sem4Subjects.name: Cybersecurity
sem4Subjects.description: Understand information security threats, data breach risk management, and cybersecurity governance frameworks for business managers.

sem4Subjects.name: Data Privacy
sem4Subjects.description: Apply data privacy regulations, GDPR-analogous Indian digital personal data protection requirements, and privacy-by-design principles in business operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Security and Governance
sem4Subjects.description: Build IT governance frameworks: information security policy, access control systems, and security audit processes for corporate compliance.

sem4Subjects.name: Tech Risk and Compliance
sem4Subjects.description: Identify and manage technology risks: third-party vendor risk, cloud infrastructure risk, and regulatory technology compliance obligations.

sem4Subjects.name: Agile Concepts
sem4Subjects.description: Apply Agile and Scrum frameworks to business project management, product development, and organisational transformation programmes.

sem4Subjects.name: Digital Product Design
sem4Subjects.description: Lead digital product development: user research, design thinking, MVP definition, and product roadmap management for digital business products.

sem4Subjects.name: Business Process Transformation
sem4Subjects.description: Drive business process reengineering, automation-enabled transformation, and change management for large-scale operational redesign.

sem4Subjects.name: AI Concepts
sem4Subjects.description: Evaluate AI applications in business management: machine learning use cases, AI product evaluation, and AI strategy for operational and competitive advantage.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1 and 2 cover the shared management core including Organisational Behaviour, Business Analytics, Strategic Management, and Operations Management. See the main NMIMS MBA page for Sem 1-2 subject details.

whoHires.body:
Business Management MBA graduates from NMIMS Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Technology consulting, BFSI digital functions, E-commerce operations, IT services, Corporate strategy

**Entry-level roles:** Business Analyst, Digital Transformation Analyst, IT Business Partner, Associate Product Manager

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Business Manager, Product Manager, Digital Transformation Manager, IT Strategy Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Business Strategy, Head of Digital, Chief Digital Officer, Managing Director (consulting practice)

**Top hiring organisations:** TCS, Infosys, Accenture, Deloitte Digital, HDFC Bank Digital, Razorpay, Zepto, Mahindra Group Innovation, Wipro Digital

skills.body:
The NMIMS Business Management specialisation builds skills that technology-forward employers test at business manager and head of digital levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Organisational Design: structure-strategy alignment, reporting layer design, and matrix organisation management for complex enterprises
- Cybersecurity and Data Governance: security risk assessment, data privacy compliance, and technology governance for business-owned digital operations
- Digital Product Management: MVP scoping, product roadmap management, and agile sprint oversight for technology product companies
- AI Concepts: evaluating AI vendor proposals, writing AI product requirements, and managing AI implementation risk for non-technical managers

**Applied skills:**
- Business Process Transformation: process mapping, RPA decision frameworks, and change management for operational digitalisation programmes
- Supply Chain Management: supply chain resilience, vendor management, and distribution strategy across business domains

**Soft skills:** Building business cases for technology investment, managing cross-functional digital transformation projects, and presenting digital strategy to non-technical boards and investors.

comparisons.body:
Comparing NMIMS Online MBA Business Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA General Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity General Management covers strategy, finance, brand management, and operations breadth. NMIMS Business Management includes technology subjects: cybersecurity, digital product design, AI concepts, and agile. For professionals at technology companies or in digital transformation roles, NMIMS is more differentiated. For traditional management roles across manufacturing and finance, Amity General Management is more targeted.

**vs Symbiosis Online MBA Operations Management:** Symbiosis is NAAC A+ at competitive fees with strong recognition for traditional business management roles. NMIMS Business Management has a technology-forward positioning that makes it more relevant for digital business and IT-adjacent management careers. Choose based on whether your target role is in digital-era business or traditional operations management.

**vs IGNOU MBA:** IGNOU is UGC-DEB approved at significantly lower fees with open enrollment. IGNOU covers general management broadly without dedicated technology management subjects. NMIMS Business Management at Rs 1.96L carries stronger employer recognition at private sector technology and BFSI companies. IGNOU MBA is better suited for government sector and PSU employment tracks.

fees.body:
The NMIMS Online MBA in Business Management offers 3 payment options:

**Option 1 — Annual payment**: Rs 1,05,000 per year, Rs 2,10,000 total for 2 years
**Option 2 — Semester-wise**: Rs 55,000 per semester, Rs 2,20,000 total for 4 semesters
**Option 3 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,96,000 one-time

An Admission Processing Fee of Rs 1,200 applies to all admissions. Rs 10,000 is collected at registration and counts toward the program fee. Examination fee is Rs 800 per subject per attempt, and project evaluation fee is Rs 800 per attempt.

For working professionals, NMIMS offers no-cost EMI plans through NBFC lending partners. Contact our counsellor for current EMI options and approval terms.

**Scholarship**: Armed Forces, Paramilitary personnel, and their dependents receive a 20 percent discount on the program fee. Submit service verification documents during admission to claim this.

Demand Draft payments should be made in favour of 'SVKM's NMIMS' payable at Mumbai.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with NMIMS each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The NMIMS (Narsee Monjee Institute of Management Studies) degree certificate carries the NMIMS name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The Business Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from NMIMS Online MBA Business Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Arjun Kapoor
items.liked: Digital product design and agile concepts modules were directly applicable to my product management role at a fintech company
items.disliked: AI concepts module was introductory — professionals managing AI products needed more advanced implementation and evaluation content
items.body: I was a business analyst moving into product management. The digital product design and agile content gave me the frameworks my engineering colleagues were already using and I was missing. The NMIMS credential was the right signal for product management — my interviewer at a leading fintech had himself done NMIMS campus MBA and validated the degree quality in the interview. Got the Associate Product Manager role within 3 months.

items.name: Sarita Mehta
items.liked: Cybersecurity and tech risk content gave me business-level understanding to manage my IT vendor relationships intelligently
items.disliked: Business process transformation module could benefit from more India-specific digital transformation case examples from BFSI sector
items.body: I manage vendor relationships and technology contracts at a large bank. The cybersecurity governance, data privacy, and tech risk content was exactly what I needed as a business manager interfacing with IT and infosec teams. The NMIMS degree gave me the management credentials for the IT Business Partner Head role. The NMIMS brand was well-recognised by our Chief Digital Officer during the promotion process.

items.name: Rohan Nair
items.liked: Business process transformation content provided a structured methodology for the operational redesign I was leading
items.disliked: Organisational design module would benefit from more case studies on tech startup scaling and org structure choices
items.body: I lead business process transformation at an IT services company. The BPT and agile content were directly applicable. The AI concepts section helped me articulate AI strategy to business stakeholders in terms they understood. The NMIMS MBA credential is recognised in Bangalore's technology industry — several of my colleagues from NMIMS campus were already in senior roles at my company.

faqs.question: Is this MBA suitable for professionals who are not in technology but work at technology companies?
faqs.answer: Yes. Business Management is specifically designed for business-side professionals at technology companies: product managers, business analysts, strategy managers, and operational leaders who need to be fluent in technology management without being software engineers. The cybersecurity, digital product, and AI subjects provide the technology fluency; the business management core provides the management foundation. This combination is exactly the profile that technology companies want in their business management roles.

faqs.question: Does the cybersecurity content lead to any security certifications?
faqs.answer: The Cybersecurity, Data Privacy, Security and Governance, and Tech Risk modules provide management-level understanding of information security rather than technical security practitioner knowledge. They do not lead to certifications like CISSP or CEH. For business managers who need to govern and audit security functions, this content is appropriate. For professionals targeting technical security roles, CISSP or CompTIA Security+ certifications are more directly applicable.

faqs.question: How relevant is the agile and digital product content for non-IT industries?
faqs.answer: Agile methodology has expanded well beyond software development into financial services, FMCG, manufacturing, and healthcare project management. Digital product design principles apply wherever companies are building digital customer experiences. For professionals in traditional industries undergoing digital transformation, this content is directly applicable. For professionals in roles where digital product development is not a current concern, the general management subjects (organisational design, supply chain, consumer behaviour) are the more immediately applicable modules.

faqs.question: What salary can NMIMS Business Management MBA graduates expect?
faqs.answer: Business managers and product managers with 3-5 years of experience who complete this MBA typically see packages of Rs 12-22 lakh at technology companies and digital transformation consulting firms. Heads of Business and digital transformation managers earn Rs 18-35 lakh. Chief Digital Officer roles at mid-size companies pay Rs 25-50 lakh. Outcomes are strongest at technology companies in Mumbai, Pune, and Bangalore where NMIMS brand resonates with hiring managers.

faqs.question: Is this spec better than NMIMS Financial Management for long-term earning potential?
faqs.answer: This comparison depends on career direction. Financial Management produces higher compensation at the peak of CFO and investment banking tracks. Business Management opens more diverse paths across technology, general management, and digital product leadership. Earning potential at senior technology company roles or COO positions is competitive with senior finance roles. Choose based on which career direction matches your existing skills and interest rather than comparing long-term theoretical salary peaks.

chooseIf: You work at a technology company or digital-first organisation in a business management, product, or strategy role and want a NMIMS credential with technology management fluency built into the curriculum.
You are driving digital transformation or business process reengineering projects and want management frameworks combined with cybersecurity, AI, and agile concepts in one degree.
You need a NIRF-ranked management credential from a brand recognised in Mumbai's technology and BFSI ecosystem that explicitly covers the modern business-technology interface.
consider: You are in a traditional industry with no digital transformation exposure — General Management or a functional spec is more relevant to your immediate career context.
You want deep technology management from the IT manager perspective — Amity IT Management or an information systems-focused programme has more technical depth.
You are specifically targeting a functional domain (finance, HR, or marketing) rather than general business management — the relevant functional spec at NMIMS is more directly aligned.
